ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "time_zone" TEXT /* Time zone of the customer */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region where the customer is located */,
  "address" TEXT /* Street address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the customer */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the customer */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code of the customer */,
  "language_preference" TEXT /* Preferred language for communication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customers_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_accounts" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user account */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user account */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date of the last login */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Status indicating whether the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_accounts_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each interaction record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer being interacted with */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who interacted with the customer */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the interaction */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., call, email, meeting) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or details about the interaction */,
  "outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the interaction (e.g., successful, pending, failed) */,
  "follow_up_date" TEXT /* Date for follow-up action */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_interactions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES user_accounts ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_interactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date the role was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contact_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each preference record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer for whom the preferences apply */,
  "preferred_contact_method" TEXT /* Preferred contact method (e.g., phone, email) */,
  "opt_in_marketing" INTEGER /* Opt-in status for marketing communications */,
  "communication_language" TEXT /* Preferred language for communication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contact_preferences_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "time_zone" TEXT /* Time zone of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marketing campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the marketing campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the marketing campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the marketing campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segmentation" (
  "segmentation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segmentation record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer being segmented */,
  "segmentation_criteria" TEXT /* Criteria for segmentation (e.g., region, language) */,
  "segmentation_value" TEXT /* Value for the segmentation criteria */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segmentation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_segmentation_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
